Remarks:
70% confident - not seen vocalising. 

Six Purple Needletail were seen that morning somewhere in this vicinity. No other Needletail species seen. 

Swiftlets and swifts could have been present but overlooked. 

This is a very short sample, just two 1 sec calls. 

ID based on above fact plus comparison with other species: 

Hirunapus have similar calls: lack of clear rising or falling pattern, almost random sample of spectrum:
- White-throated: XC157710
- Silver-backed: XC611446
- Brown-backed: ML181328281 

Lack of similarity to available swiftlet flight calls (noting that not many recordings available):
- Grey-rumped Swiftlet 
- Ridgetop Swiftlet 
- Ameline Swiftlet 

Only other possibility I can find is Pacific Swift, e.g. XC286311. Pacific Swifts have a strong falling pattern. 

Ridgetop road through primary forest at 400-500m a.s.l.
